_meta.extraction_method: PyMuPDF text layer + manual image reading of scanned pages
notes: TEC values read from scanned image (page 70 of PDF) - verify against physical rulebook
CART (Anti-Armor Fire) read from scanned image (page 15)
Prose rules extracted from OCR text layer (34MB PDF)
Section index sourced from TOC on page 2 - actual page numbers may differ by ~1

terrain_effects_chart._note: Values read from scanned image. L = column shift left (defender advantage). + = add to base terrain cost.

columns.cp_non_mot: CP cost for non-motorized units to enter or cross
columns.cp_mot: CP cost for motorized units to enter or cross
columns.combat_barrage: Column shift on Barrage Combat Results Table
columns.combat_anti_armor: Column shift on Anti-Armor Fire Results Table
columns.combat_close_assault: Column shift on Close Assault Results Table
columns.stacking_limit: Maximum Stacking Points allowed in hex

notes: note2: most vehicles prohibited, only via track
note3: light trucks/recce/motorcycle may not enter
note1: garrison units 0 stacking space, unlimited AA units at 0 space
note4: Alexandria and Cairo are Level 3 Fortifications
note12: armor defensive benefit only in Major City hex
note5: Commonwealth Player may use Rail Movement (8.7)
note10*: BD value of 10 applies only when leaving the rail line
note6: negates all hexside terrain entry costs and BD point values
Cost reduces base terrain cost by 1
note7: halves all terrain feature entry costs and hex/hexside BD values except CP expended and BD from moving vehicle down escarpment
note8: vehicles may use track to cross escarpment downward only
Hexside feature — adds to base terrain cost
note: no vehicle may ever move UP an escarpment (8.42)
Via Track down only: +8 CP, 6 BD Points (8.42)
Via Track: +8 CP, 6 BD (8.42)
note10: motorized may only cross on a track during rainstorm unless road/railroad present
note11: may only be crossed if hexside crossed by road or railroad
note10: may only be crossed during Rainstorm if road/railroad present (2 additional CP)
note12: armor targets defensive benefit only in Major City hex
Alexandria and Cairo are Level 3 by default (note4)
note13: engineer units reduce cost to enter (26.2)
note13: if assaulting forces are in enemy minefield, non-phasing forces receive L1 for AA and CA if not already receiving for friendly minefield

combat_system._note: CNA uses a percentage-based combat system, NOT a traditional odds-ratio CRT.
combat_system.overview: Combat strength is expressed in Raw Points. A percentage of loss is determined from the relevant results table (based on dice roll and column shifts). That percentage is applied to total Raw Points to get actual casualties.

barrage.description: Indirect fire by artillery. Attacker determines Actual Barrage Points, selects targets. Resolves on Barrage Combat Results Table. Subtracts ammunition.
barrage.modifiers: Column shifts from terrain (TEC), supply status, cohesion

anti_armor_fire.description: Anti-tank fire against armor. Resolves on Anti-Armor Fire Combat Results Table.
anti_armor_fire.die: d66 (two d6 read as tens/ones giving 11-66)
anti_armor_fire.result_type: % of Anti-Armor Points lost by target
anti_armor_fire.table_structure: Row = d66 result (11-66, skipping 7/8/9), Column = Anti-Armor Points (0* to 16+)

close_assault.description: Direct combat between adjacent units. Both sides compute Raw Points. Phasing Player may announce Close Assault or Overrun.
modifiers: Organizational size difference (15.53): +1 to +8 column shifts based on size disparity

organizational_size_adjustments.note: Adjustments always in favor of larger unit size

sequence: 1. Phasing Player announces hexes being assaulted
2. Both Players compute Raw Points (TOE Strength x Combat Rating)
6. Resolve on Close Assault Results Table
7. Apply losses (attacker rounds up, defender rounds down — 15.53c)

anti_armor_results_table._note: Read from scanned image. Row = d66 die roll. Column = Anti-Armor Points. Cell = % of TOE lost.

capability_point_system.description: All actions in CNA cost Capability Points (CPs). Each unit has a Capability Point Allowance (CPA) per Operations Stage. Moving costs CPs per hex (per TEC). Combat costs CPs. Exceeding CPA earns Disorganization Points.

cohesion.description: Cohesion Level (CL) represents exhaustion/elation. CL=0 is normal. Negative CL = Disorganization. Positive CL = Reorganization.
cohesion.disorganization_threshold: When a unit exceeds its CPA, it earns Disorganization Points (DPs)
cohesion.effect_on_combat: Cohesion Level affects Morale Level at instant of combat

stacking_rules.general: Each hex has a maximum Stacking Point limit. Units have Stacking Point values based on their organizational size.

stacking_point_values._note: Read from scanned image (partially OCR'd). Values are per unit equivalent.

special_cases.garrison_units: Garrison unit in assigned hex occupies 0 stacking space (TEC note 1)
special_cases.aa_units_in_city: Unlimited AA units in Major City hex at 0 stacking space (TEC note 1)
special_cases.aa_units_airfield: 3 AA units at Airfield hex at 0 stacking space (TEC note 1)

stacking_rules.hex_limit_by_terrain: See TEC (8.37) — generally 6 Stacking Points per hex

zoc_rules.general: Units with Assault Points exert a Zone of Control (ZOC) into all 6 adjacent hexes, with exceptions.
exceptions: ZOCs do not extend through All-Sea hexsides (10.21a)
ZOCs do not extend through Major River hexsides (10.21a)
ZOCs do not extend through Lake hexsides (10.21a)
ZOCs do not extend through Escarpment hexsides (10.21b)
ZOCs do not extend into hexes the unit could not enter from its present location (10.21c)
zoc_rules.anti_armor_exception: Units assigned to Anti-Armor role do NOT exert a ZOC (10.15)
zoc_rules.entry_cost: No CP cost to enter an enemy ZOC (10.22)
zoc_rules.stopping: Unit must cease movement upon entering an enemy-controlled hex (10.23)
zoc_rules.mutual_disclosure: Non-Phasing Player must inform Phasing Player whether a unit exerts ZOC into a hex the Phasing Player enters or begins in (10.16)

movement_rules.general: Units move hex by hex paying CP costs per TEC. Movement must be consecutive; units may not skip hexes.
movement_rules.involuntary_movement: Retreats also require CP expenditure and Breakdown checks (8.12)
movement_rules.prohibition: A unit may never enter a hex containing an enemy unit (8.13, except 27.4)

breakdown.description: Moving vehicles accumulate Breakdown Points (BPs). When BPs equal or exceed unit's Breakdown Rating, unit becomes broken down.
breakdown.escarpment_special: No vehicle may ever move UP an escarpment. Via Track downward: +8 CP, 6 BD Points (8.42)

movement_rules.road_movement: Road reduces terrain entry cost by 1 CP and negates hexside feature costs and BD (TEC note 6)
movement_rules.track_movement: Track halves terrain feature costs and BD values; vehicles may use to cross escarpment downward only (TEC note 7/8)

railroad_movement.direction: Each player may use rail lines once per direction per game-turn
railroad_movement.limit: One unit or stack per direction, any distance

fuel.general: Almost every vehicle consumes fuel when moving. Players track fuel on Convoy Sheets.
fuel.consumption: Fuel consumed equals CPs expended by motorized units

evaporation.source: Per-OpStage loss from fuel stored in drums (British) vs jerry cans (all others)

fuel.transport: Rule 49.2 covers transportation of fuel by truck

ammunition.general: Combat units carry enough ammo to fire once without resupply (50.1)
ammunition.consumption_chart: See Ammunition Consumption Rates Chart (Charts and Tables section)
ammunition.note: Combat units may carry enough ammo to fire once before needing resupply from dumps

stores.definition: Everything that isn't fuel, ammunition, or water — food, paper, uniforms, etc.
stores.distribution: Distributed at beginning of Game-Turn (not each OpStage)
stores.consumption_rate: 4 Stores Points per TOE Strength Point per Game-Turn (51.11)
stores.prisoner_rate: 1 Stores Point per 5 Prisoner Points per OpStage (51.12). Prisoners supplied before all other units.

water.without_water_penalty: Each infantry unit loses 1 TOE Point for every consecutive OpStage after the first without water (52.53)

italian_pasta_rule.full_text: One of the biggest mistakes the Italians made during the entire Desert Campaign was to provide their troops with a diet which was composed, in large part, of spaghetti and macaroni. Aside from providing insufficient protein (this wasn't Buitoni Brand), pasta has one serious drawback in the desert: you need water to cook it! Therefore, each Italian battalion, when it receives its Stores, must receive an additional 1 Point of Water when Stores are distributed. Any battalion-sized unit that does not receive their Pasta Point (one Water point) may not voluntarily exceed their CPA that Turn. Furthermore, Italian battalions not receiving their Pasta Point that have a Cohesion Level of -10 or worse immediately become Disorganized, as if they had reached -26. As soon as such units get their Pasta Point, they regain the original Cohesion Level (i.e., the level they had before they disintegrated).
effect_no_pasta: Cannot voluntarily exceed CPA that Turn
If Cohesion Level <= -10: immediately become Disorganized as if at -26
italian_pasta_rule.recovery: Unit regains pre-disorganization Cohesion Level as soon as it receives its Pasta Point

phases: II. Strategic Air Planning Stage (Air Game Only)
A. Designation Phase: assign planes to Land Support or Strategic missions
B. Axis Malta Availability Determination Phase
E. Naval Convoy Resolution Phase (Air Game Only) [Segments 1 & 2]
III. Naval Convoy Arrival Phase: all convoy cargo placed at port of arrival
6. Supply Distribution Segment: distribute supplies in each Supply Zone
7. Tactical Shipping Segment: transport units/supply by sea
VI. Strategic Air Recovery Stage (Air Game Only)
VII-IX. First, Second, Third Operations Stages (each identical):
a. Air Availability Phase (Air Game Only)
e. Refueling / Rearming Phase (Air Game Only)
i. Player A's Phase (Movement then Combat in any order)
iv. Fuel Evaporation Phase: adjust fuel levels for evaporation
v. Patrol Phase (if no combat this OpStage)

key_section_page_map._note: Verified page numbers from PDF text search. Section index TOC entries all appear on p2 of text PDF (the TOC page). Actual content pages listed here.
